original_hypothesis.title: Hooks Fallback Delegatecall Enables Arbitrary Storage Manipulation by Malicious Hook
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Hooks.sol fallback performs delegatecall to hook implementations with full msg.data. The hook executes in the wallet's storage context with no restrictions on storage access. A malicious hook can overwrite the implementation slot (sstore(address(), attacker_impl)), the imageHash (IMAGE_HASH_KEY), nonce values, reentrancy guard, or any other storage slot. This grants total wallet control. While hook installation requires onlySelf authorization, a wallet owner who installs a seemingly benign hook with a hidden backdoor would lose all assets.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The fallback at Hooks.sol delegates to any registered hook with no storage access restrictions. The delegatecall grants the hook the same privileges as the wallet implementation itself.

proof_of_signal.pattern: delegatecall(msg.data) to registered hook with no storage access restrictions

preconditions: Wallet owner installs a malicious or compromised hook via addHook
External caller invokes the hook's selector on the wallet

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: (bool success, bytes memory result) = target.delegatecall(msg.data); with target from storage
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Hook has unrestricted storage write access in wallet context
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No storage slot restrictions, no immutable slot protection, no hook sandboxing

analysis_questions: Can a hook overwrite the implementation slot to hijack the proxy?
Can a hook modify IMAGE_HASH_KEY to change signer configuration?
Is there any hook verification or auditing mechanism?
validation_requirements: Create a malicious hook that overwrites the implementation slot
Demonstrate wallet takeover via hook-modified storage
Verify no storage protection exists for critical slots

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Wallet owner installs hook with selector 0xdeadbeef. Hook contract contains: function handleDeadbeef() { assembly { sstore(address(), attacker_implementation) } }. Attacker calls wallet with selector 0xdeadbeef. Hook executes via delegatecall, overwrites implementation. All future calls to wallet are handled by attacker's implementation.
prerequisites: Wallet owner must install the malicious hook via onlySelf

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete wallet takeover - all assets drainable
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: The specific wallet with malicious hook installed

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test verifying hooks cannot modify critical storage slots

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: DefinedHook event emitted at installation; delegatecall storage writes not logged

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
